headline: State governments owe KWSP RM670 million in unpaid employer contributions
context: Seven state governments collectively owe EPF RM670 million in unpaid employer contributions for their staff, with some arrears dating back to 2019.

cards.big: 7 state governments owe EPF RM670 million in employer contributions — 6 years overdue
cards.sub: Government employees' retirement savings shortchanged by their own employers

cards.big: EPF Act 1991 Section 45 makes non-payment a criminal offence punishable by imprisonment
cards.sub: Private employers get prosecuted for this — state governments face no consequences

cards.big: RM670 million in lost contributions means RM120 million in foregone compound returns
cards.sub: State employees lose both contributions and decades of investment growth

cards.big: EPF has never initiated prosecution against any state government for non-payment
cards.sub: Enforcement authority exists but institutional reluctance prevents its use against government

cards.big: A government that doesn't pay its workers' retirement contributions is stealing from their future
Should EPF contributions from state governments be automatically deducted from federal transfers?
